title: DMAE
description: DMAE (2-dimethylaminoethanol) is a compound structurally similar to choline that may serve as a precursor to acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory and cognitive function. It has been studied for potential cognitive enhancement, anti-aging effects, and has been used topically in skincare formulations. DMAE is available as a dietary supplement and has been investigated for various neurological and dermatological applications.

research.title: Effects of dimethylaminoethanol pyroglutamate (DMAE p-Glu) against memory deficits induced by scopolamine: evidence from preclinical and clinical studies
research.summary: DMAE pyroglutamate demonstrated procholinergic and promnesic effects in both animal models and human studies, showing potential for improving cognitive deficits related to cholinergic dysfunction.

research.title: In vivo skin effects of a dimethylaminoethanol (DMAE) based formulation
research.summary: Topical DMAE application increased dermal thickness and collagen fiber thickness in mice, and improved skin hydration in human volunteers, suggesting potential anti-aging benefits for skin.

research.title: New insights on dimethylaminoethanol (DMAE) features as a free radical scavenger
research.summary: DMAE demonstrated ability to scavenge hydroxyl, ascorbyl, and lipid radicals in vitro, supporting its potential role as an antioxidant compound.

research.title: Use of DMAE (2-dimethylaminoethanol) in the induction of lucid dreams
research.summary: DMAE was proposed as a supplement that may facilitate the induction of lucid dreams, though this was a hypothesis paper rather than a controlled study.

research.title: Dimethylaminoethanol affects the viability of human cultured fibroblasts
research.summary: Study found that DMAE directly affected cultured human fibroblasts, raising questions about its mechanism of action beyond simple muscle contraction theories.

research.title: Effects of dimethylaminoethanol and compound amino acid on D-galactose induced skin aging model of rat
research.summary: DMAE and amino acid supplementation showed antiaging effects on skin in a rat model of accelerated aging, potentially through antioxidant mechanisms.

research.title: Comparative disposition of dimethylaminoethanol and choline in rats and mice following oral or intravenous administration
research.summary: Pharmacokinetic study characterizing the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of DMAE in rodents, providing safety and toxicology data.

sentiment.summary: User experiences with DMAE are genuinely divided, with some reporting mild cognitive benefits or use in pre-workout stacks, while many others report negligible effects or find it ineffective compared to expectations. Most users don't consider it a standout nootropic.

effects.label: Subtle or Absent Effects:
effects.description: Many users report negligible or very subtle effects from DMAE supplementation, with effectiveness often questioned compared to other nootropics

effects.label: Better in Combinations:
effects.description: When effects are noticed, they tend to occur when DMAE is stacked with other supplements rather than used alone

effects.label: Individual Variability:
effects.description: Response appears highly individual, with some users finding mild benefits while others experience no noticeable changes

effects.label: Not a Primary Nootropic:
effects.description: Generally not considered a first-line or standout nootropic supplement by experienced users, often overshadowed by other options

dosage.label: Standard Range:
dosage.description: Commonly mentioned doses range from 100-1200mg daily, with lower doses around 100-500mg being more typical

dosage.label: Pre-Workout Use:
dosage.description: When used in pre-workout contexts, doses are typically included as part of larger formulations rather than standalone

dosage.label: Third of Recommended:
dosage.description: Some users report taking only a third of the recommended dosage when stacking with other choline sources

dosage.label: Timing Considerations:
dosage.description: Generally taken in the morning or before activities requiring focus, avoided in evening due to potential stimulant effects

sideEffects.label: Choline Overload:
sideEffects.description: When combined with multiple choline sources (Alpha-GPC, CDP-Choline), users report potential for depression and attention issues

sideEffects.label: Minimal Standalone Effects:
sideEffects.description: Most common complaint is lack of noticeable effects rather than negative side effects

sideEffects.label: Topical Skin Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Some research suggests concentrated topical DMAE may affect fibroblast viability, raising questions about skincare applications

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: When side effects occur, they tend to be mild, with most users reporting no adverse reactions at standard doses

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: DMAE is readily available as a dietary supplement from various online retailers and supplement companies

availability.label: Part of Formulations:
availability.description: Often found as an ingredient in pre-workout supplements and nootropic blends rather than purchased standalone

availability.label: Quality Concerns:
availability.description: Users recommend purchasing from reputable brands with third-party testing, though standalone DMAE is less commonly purchased than other nootropics
